scenarios.title: Dispute Subcontractor Invoice for Incomplete Work
scenarios.description: A project manager receives an invoice from a subcontractor for work that is only 50% complete. The invoice must be disputed.

scenarios.title: Architect Responds to RFI
scenarios.description: An architect needs to respond to an RFI about a wall dimension discrepancy.

scenarios.title: Client Approves a Change Order
scenarios.description: A client reviews a change order for a new patio and approves it.

scenarios.title: Verify Subcontractor's Insurance Certificate
scenarios.description: Before a new plumbing subcontractor can start work, the contract manager must verify they have submitted a valid certificate of insurance (COI).

scenarios.title: Client Rejects a Change Order
scenarios.description: A client reviews a change order and rejects it due to the high cost.

scenarios.title: Issue Notice to Proceed
scenarios.description: After signing a contract with a new subcontractor, the contract manager must issue a formal Notice to Proceed (NTP).

scenarios.title: Process Final Payment and Release of Retainage
scenarios.description: The project is complete and all punch list items are closed. The accountant must process the final payment to a subcontractor, including the release of held retainage.

scenarios.title: Submit Steel Shop Drawings for Review
scenarios.description: The steel fabricator has completed their shop drawings. The project manager must submit them to the structural engineer for review and approval.

scenarios.title: Review Submittal Returned 'As Noted'
scenarios.description: An engineer reviews shop drawings and returns them with the status 'Approved as Noted'. The PM must review the notes and distribute them.

scenarios.title: Process a Subcontractor Lien Waiver
scenarios.description: Along with their monthly invoice, a subcontractor has submitted a conditional lien waiver. The accountant must process it.

scenarios.title: Request Final Building Inspection
scenarios.description: The project is nearing completion. The PM must formally request the final inspection from the city's building department to obtain the Certificate of Occupancy.

scenarios.title: Negotiate Change Order Cost
scenarios.description: A subcontractor has submitted a change order proposal that the PM believes is overpriced. The PM needs to negotiate a lower price.

scenarios.title: Send Formal Notice of Delay
scenarios.description: Due to a permitting delay caused by the city, the PM must send a formal notice to the client to protect the company's contractual rights regarding the schedule.

scenarios.title: Manage a Joint Check Agreement
scenarios.description: A subcontractor is having financial trouble, so a joint check agreement is put in place with their material supplier. The accountant must process a payment accordingly.

scenarios.title: Close Out the Submittal Log
scenarios.description: As part of project closeout, a project engineer must ensure every item in the submittal log has been reviewed and closed.

scenarios.title: Check Subcontractor Bonding Capacity
scenarios.description: Before awarding a large subcontract, a contract manager must verify that the subcontractor has sufficient bonding capacity.

scenarios.title: Request a Time Extension
scenarios.description: Due to a client-caused delay, a PM needs to submit a formal request for a contract time extension.

scenarios.title: Process a Conditional Payment Notice
scenarios.description: A general contractor receives a notice from a sub-subcontractor that they have not been paid by the subcontractor. The GC must now take action to ensure payment.

scenarios.title: Assemble Project Closeout Package
scenarios.description: At the end of a project, a project engineer must assemble the final closeout package for the client, including all warranties and O&M manuals.

scenarios.title: Create Daily Site Log
scenarios.description: A site superintendent needs to create the daily log for the day's activities.

scenarios.title: Order Drywall for Interior Walls
scenarios.description: A foreman needs to order more drywall for the upcoming interior wall installation.

scenarios.title: Log a Paint Defect for Punch List
scenarios.description: During a final walkthrough, a quality inspector finds an issue with the paint finish in the main lobby and needs to add it to the punch list.

scenarios.title: Verify Plumbing Rough-in Inspection
scenarios.description: A project manager needs to verify that the plumbing rough-in has passed the city inspection before the drywall crew can start.

scenarios.title: Schedule Concrete Pour and Testing
scenarios.description: A site manager needs to schedule a concrete pour for the building's foundation and coordinate the required third-party slump test.

scenarios.title: Manage Site Visitor Access
scenarios.description: A client representative is visiting the site. The site superintendent must log their entry and ensure they have the required PPE.

scenarios.title: Document As-Built Condition
scenarios.description: During construction, a pipe had to be rerouted from its planned location. A foreman must document this change on the as-built drawings.

scenarios.title: Schedule Routine Equipment Maintenance
scenarios.description: A notification indicates that a backhoe is due for its 250-hour service. The site manager must schedule this maintenance.

scenarios.title: Reject Damaged Material Delivery
scenarios.description: A delivery of windows arrives on site, but the foreman notices several are cracked. The delivery must be rejected.

scenarios.title: Close Out a Punch List Item
scenarios.description: A subcontractor has fixed a paint defect. The quality inspector must verify the fix and close the punch list item.

scenarios.title: Coordinate a Critical Crane Lift
scenarios.description: A site manager must coordinate a critical lift of a large HVAC unit onto the roof, which requires temporarily closing a section of the site.

scenarios.title: Track Concrete Curing Time
scenarios.description: After a concrete slab is poured, the site manager must track its curing time and test results before allowing heavy equipment on it.

scenarios.title: Request a Dumpster Swap-Out
scenarios.description: The construction debris dumpster on site is full. The site superintendent needs to request a swap-out from the waste management company.

scenarios.title: Photo-document Project Progress
scenarios.description: A project engineer needs to take weekly progress photos from established photo points around the site.

scenarios.title: Assign Task to a Crew
scenarios.description: A foreman needs to formally assign the 'Install 1st Floor Windows' task to their crew in the task management system.

scenarios.title: Conduct On-Site Material Inventory Check
scenarios.description: A foreman needs to check the on-site inventory of copper pipe to ensure there is enough for the next week's plumbing work.

scenarios.title: Conduct Pre-Task Safety Briefing
scenarios.description: Before starting a concrete demolition task, a foreman must conduct and document a pre-task safety briefing with the crew.

scenarios.title: Sign Out a Piece of Equipment
scenarios.description: A carpenter needs to use a power saw from the on-site tool crib. The tool crib manager must log the checkout.

scenarios.title: Conduct Final Walkthrough with Client
scenarios.description: The project is substantially complete. The PM needs to conduct the final walkthrough with the client to generate the initial punch list.

scenarios.title: Coordinate a Utility Shutdown
scenarios.description: To tie in the new water line, the water supply to the existing building must be shut down for four hours. The site manager must coordinate this.

scenarios.title: Report Theft of Materials
scenarios.description: A foreman arrives on site in the morning and discovers a pallet of copper wire has been stolen overnight.

scenarios.title: Verify Installation Against Specifications
scenarios.description: A quality inspector must verify that the newly installed windows match the model number specified in the project's technical specifications.

scenarios.title: Manage Concrete Test Cylinder Samples
scenarios.description: During a concrete pour, a technician takes test cylinders. The site manager must ensure they are correctly labeled and stored before being picked up by the lab.

scenarios.title: Manage Site Dewatering Operations
scenarios.description: After heavy rain, an excavation is filled with water. The superintendent must manage dewatering operations in compliance with environmental permits.

scenarios.title: Implement Winter Weather Precautions
scenarios.description: With freezing temperatures forecast, a superintendent must implement winter weather precautions to protect the site and materials.

scenarios.title: Project Schedule Delay Due to Weather
scenarios.description: A project manager must update the project schedule to account for three days of lost work due to severe weather.

scenarios.title: Allocate Excavator to Foundation Work
scenarios.description: A site manager needs to allocate an available excavator to the foundation excavation task for the next five days.

scenarios.title: Assess Risk of Supply Chain Delay
scenarios.description: A project manager identifies a potential supply chain delay for custom windows and must assess the impact.

scenarios.title: Track Concrete Cost Overrun
scenarios.description: A project manager notices that concrete costs are trending over budget and needs to analyze the variance.

scenarios.title: Reallocate Crew Due to Material Delay
scenarios.description: The delivery of structural steel is delayed by a week. The project manager must reallocate the steel erection crew to another task.

scenarios.title: Forecast Final Project Cost
scenarios.description: At the 50% completion mark, a project manager needs to forecast the final project cost based on performance to date.

scenarios.title: Resolve Task Dependency Conflict
scenarios.description: The project schedule shows that 'painting' is scheduled to start before 'drywall finishing' is complete. This logical conflict must be resolved.

scenarios.title: Assess Schedule Impact of a Change Order
scenarios.description: A client-approved change order to add a new room will add 10 days of work. A PM must assess the impact on the project's completion date.

scenarios.title: Request Additional Labor to Accelerate Schedule
scenarios.description: To get back on schedule after a delay, a project manager needs to request two additional carpenters for the framing crew for one week.

scenarios.title: Create a High-Level Milestone Schedule
scenarios.description: For a client presentation, a project manager needs to create a high-level schedule showing only major project milestones.

scenarios.title: Analyze Resource Over-allocation
scenarios.description: A project manager sees an alert that the lead electrician is allocated to work 16 hours in a single day. This over-allocation must be resolved.

scenarios.title: Compress Schedule Using Fast-Tracking
scenarios.description: To shorten the project duration, a PM decides to use fast-tracking by starting the electrical rough-in while structural steel work is still ongoing.

scenarios.title: Update Budget for Material Price Increase
scenarios.description: A supplier has announced a 10% price increase for lumber. The PM must update the project budget to reflect this.

scenarios.title: Resolve Issue of Unforeseen Obstruction
scenarios.description: During excavation, a large, undocumented utility line is discovered. The PM must manage this issue.

scenarios.title: Update Project Schedule from Daily Log
scenarios.description: A PM reviews the daily log and updates the main project schedule based on the reported progress.

scenarios.title: Prepare for Weekly OAC Meeting
scenarios.description: A PM needs to prepare the agenda and supporting documents for the weekly Owner-Architect-Contractor (OAC) meeting.

scenarios.title: Level Resources to Smooth Workload
scenarios.description: A PM uses resource leveling to resolve over-allocations by delaying non-critical tasks to smooth out the workload for the carpentry crew.

scenarios.title: Create a Work Breakdown Structure (WBS)
scenarios.description: For a new residential home project, a PM needs to create an initial Work Breakdown Structure (WBS).

scenarios.title: Assess Risk of a Labor Strike
scenarios.description: News reports indicate a potential labor strike by local union electricians. A PM must assess the risk to the project.

scenarios.title: Update Budget with Committed Costs
scenarios.description: A purchase order for steel has been issued. The PM must update the budget to reflect this 'committed cost'.

scenarios.title: Archive a Completed Project
scenarios.description: A project has been completed and all payments settled. The PM must formally archive the project.

scenarios.title: Compare Current Schedule to Baseline
scenarios.description: A PM wants to understand the project's schedule variance by comparing the current schedule to the original baseline schedule.

scenarios.title: Create a Project Cash Flow Projection
scenarios.description: A PM needs to create a cash flow projection to show the expected timing of expenses and client payments throughout the project.

scenarios.title: Identify Schedule Float
scenarios.description: A PM needs to identify which upcoming tasks have float (can be delayed without affecting the project completion date) to allow for resource reallocation.

scenarios.title: Release Labor Resources
scenarios.description: The framing phase is complete. The PM needs to release the framing crew from the project so they can be assigned elsewhere.

scenarios.title: Resolve Stakeholder Disagreement
scenarios.description: The architect and the structural engineer disagree on a design detail. The PM must facilitate a resolution.

scenarios.title: Create a Recovery Schedule
scenarios.description: After significant delays, a project is 30 days behind schedule. The PM must create a recovery schedule to make up the lost time.

scenarios.title: Manage Spending Against an Allowance
scenarios.description: The contract includes a $20,000 allowance for light fixtures. The PM must help the client make selections while staying within this allowance.

scenarios.title: Conduct a Project Lessons Learned Meeting
scenarios.description: After a project is complete, the PM holds a 'lessons learned' meeting with the team to identify successes and failures to improve future projects.

scenarios.title: Report Minor Safety Incident
scenarios.description: A safety officer needs to report a minor incident where a worker twisted their ankle.

scenarios.title: Check Electrical Permit Status
scenarios.description: A project manager needs to check the status of the electrical permit before starting the wiring work.

scenarios.title: Generate Monthly OSHA 300 Log
scenarios.description: A safety manager needs to generate the OSHA 300 log of work-related injuries and illnesses for the previous month.

scenarios.title: Create JHA for Roofing Work
scenarios.description: A safety officer must create a Job Hazard Analysis (JHA) for the upcoming roofing installation.

scenarios.title: Document a Weekly Toolbox Talk
scenarios.description: A foreman conducts a weekly toolbox talk on ladder safety and needs to log it for compliance.

scenarios.title: Verify Crane Operator Certification
scenarios.description: Before a critical lift, the safety officer must verify that the crane operator's certification is valid and on file.

scenarios.title: Manage Stormwater Pollution Prevention Plan (SWPPP) Inspection
scenarios.description: A government inspector is on site to conduct a SWPPP inspection. The safety manager must provide the required documentation.

scenarios.title: Identify Unsafe Excavation Hazard
scenarios.description: A safety officer on a site walk notices an excavation over 5 feet deep that lacks proper shoring or sloping.

scenarios.title: Update Safety Data Sheet (SDS) Binder
scenarios.description: A new type of sealant is being used on site. The safety manager must add its Safety Data Sheet (SDS) to the project's chemical safety binder.

scenarios.title: Respond to Neighborhood Noise Complaint
scenarios.description: The project receives a noise complaint from a neighbor. The site manager must verify compliance with local noise ordinances.

scenarios.title: Log a Near-Miss Incident
scenarios.description: A worker dropped a tool from scaffolding, but no one was injured. A safety officer needs to log this as a 'near-miss' to prevent future occurrences.

scenarios.title: Check for Hot Work Permit
scenarios.description: A foreman sees a welder preparing to work and must first ensure a hot work permit has been issued for the area.

scenarios.title: Investigate Missing Scaffolding Tag
scenarios.description: A safety officer notices a scaffold that is missing its inspection tag (green or red). They must investigate its safety status.

scenarios.title: Document an EPA Inspection
scenarios.description: An inspector from the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) conducts a site visit. The safety manager must document the visit and any findings.

scenarios.title: Address an OSHA Complaint
scenarios.description: The company receives a formal complaint from OSHA regarding inadequate fall protection on a specific site. The safety director must respond.

scenarios.title: Create a Site-Specific Safety Plan
scenarios.description: For a new project, a safety director must create a site-specific safety plan.

scenarios.title: Conduct Post-Abatement Air Quality Test
scenarios.description: After asbestos abatement work, a safety officer must ensure an independent air quality test is performed and passed before allowing other workers into the area.

scenarios.title: Update Emergency Action Plan
scenarios.description: Due to new construction nearby, the primary evacuation route has changed. The safety manager must update the project's Emergency Action Plan (EAP).

scenarios.title: Manage Site Dust Control
scenarios.description: On a windy day, the site is generating a lot of dust. The superintendent must implement dust control measures to comply with environmental regulations.

scenarios.title: File Annual OSHA Injury Summary
scenarios.description: It's the end of the calendar year. A safety director must compile and electronically file the OSHA Form 300A, the summary of work-related injuries and illnesses.

scenarios.title: Manage Silica Dust Exposure Monitoring
scenarios.description: During rock drilling operations, a safety officer must ensure compliance with OSHA's silica dust regulations.

scenarios.title: Draft Project Proposal from Client Brief
scenarios.description: An agent must draft a project proposal based on a client's initial project brief document.

scenarios.title: Conduct Competitor Analysis
scenarios.description: An agent must research and summarize the strengths and weaknesses of three key competitors identified by the client.

scenarios.title: Analyze Client Financial Data
scenarios.description: An agent must analyze a client's financial statements (CSV) to identify trends in profitability and liquidity.

scenarios.title: Create a SWOT Analysis
scenarios.description: Based on a set of internal documents and market research reports, an agent must create a SWOT (Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, Threats) analysis for the client.

scenarios.title: Create Presentation from Final Report
scenarios.description: An agent must create a 10-slide executive presentation based on a detailed final report document.

scenarios.title: Create Statement of Work (SOW)
scenarios.description: Using an approved proposal and a standard SOW template, an agent must create a formal Statement of Work.

scenarios.title: Summarize Stakeholder Interview Transcripts
scenarios.description: An agent must process transcripts from five stakeholder interviews and summarize key themes and pain points.

scenarios.title: Create a Process Map
scenarios.description: Based on a workshop transcript describing a client's procurement process, an agent must create a visual process map.

scenarios.title: Brainstorm Cost-Saving Initiatives
scenarios.description: Given a client's main expense categories, an agent must brainstorm a list of potential cost-saving initiatives.

scenarios.title: Write an Executive Summary
scenarios.description: An agent must write a one-page executive summary of a 50-page detailed analysis report.

scenarios.title: Identify Project Risks
scenarios.description: Based on a project plan, an agent must identify potential risks and suggest mitigation strategies.

scenarios.title: Market Sizing Research
scenarios.description: An agent must research and estimate the Total Addressable Market (TAM) for a new product, citing its sources.

scenarios.title: Create a Financial Forecast
scenarios.description: Using historical sales data and a set of growth assumptions, an agent must create a 5-year revenue forecast.

scenarios.title: Develop Implementation Roadmap
scenarios.description: After a strategy has been decided, an agent must create a phased implementation roadmap.

scenarios.title: Draft Weekly Client Update Email
scenarios.description: An agent must draft a weekly status update email for a client based on a list of completed tasks and upcoming activities.

scenarios.title: Create a Resource Allocation Plan
scenarios.description: For a new project, an agent must create a resource plan, assigning consultants from a list based on their skills and availability.

scenarios.title: Clean and Prepare Client Data
scenarios.description: An agent must clean a raw client dataset (CSV) by handling missing values and removing duplicates before analysis.

scenarios.title: Perform Customer Segmentation
scenarios.description: Using customer purchase data, an agent must perform a simple RFM (Recency, Frequency, Monetary) segmentation.

scenarios.title: Build vs. Buy Analysis
scenarios.description: A client needs a new CRM system. An agent must create a simple build vs. buy analysis.

scenarios.title: Create Meeting Minutes from Transcript
scenarios.description: An agent must generate structured meeting minutes from a raw audio transcript of a client meeting.

scenarios.title: Create a Project Budget
scenarios.description: Based on a resource plan and standard consultant day rates, an agent must create a project budget.

scenarios.title: Industry Trend Analysis
scenarios.description: An agent must analyze a collection of recent news articles and reports to identify the top 3 emerging trends in the retail industry.

scenarios.title: Benchmark Client KPIs
scenarios.description: An agent must compare a client's Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) against industry benchmarks.

scenarios.title: Porter's Five Forces Analysis
scenarios.description: An agent must conduct a Porter's Five Forces analysis for a client in the airline industry based on provided research documents.

scenarios.title: Redact Sensitive Information from Report
scenarios.description: Before sharing a report externally, an agent must redact all personally identifiable information (PII) and client-confidential terms.

scenarios.title: Validate Proposal Against Template
scenarios.description: An agent must check a draft proposal to ensure all mandatory sections from the company's standard template are included.

scenarios.title: Extract Table Data from PDF Reports
scenarios.description: An agent must extract specific data tables from a collection of PDF market research reports.

scenarios.title: Perform Sensitivity Analysis
scenarios.description: Given a financial model, an agent must perform a sensitivity analysis by changing key input variables.

scenarios.title: Prioritize Recommendations
scenarios.description: Given a list of strategic recommendations, an agent must prioritize them using an Impact/Effort matrix.

scenarios.title: Translate Report for International Client
scenarios.description: An agent must translate a final report from English to Spanish for a client's international office.

scenarios.title: Resolve Staffing Conflict
scenarios.description: Two project proposals request the same lead data scientist. An agent must identify the conflict and propose a solution.

scenarios.title: Create a Customer Survey Questionnaire
scenarios.description: To gather customer feedback for a client, an agent must create a survey questionnaire.

scenarios.title: Visualize Survey Results
scenarios.description: An agent must take raw survey result data (CSV) and create visualizations to highlight the findings.

scenarios.title: Develop a Change Management Plan
scenarios.description: For a major technology implementation project, an agent must draft a change management plan.

scenarios.title: Create a Project Glossary
scenarios.description: An agent must scan a collection of project documents and create a glossary of key terms and acronyms.

scenarios.title: Estimate Project Timeline
scenarios.description: Given a list of high-level tasks, an agent must provide a rough time estimate for a project.

scenarios.title: Find an Expert for Interview
scenarios.description: An agent must find three potential industry experts on a niche topic ('cold chain logistics for pharmaceuticals') to be interviewed for a project.

scenarios.title: Cost-Benefit Analysis
scenarios.description: An agent must perform a cost-benefit analysis for a proposed IT project.

scenarios.title: Formulate Strategic Options
scenarios.description: Based on a SWOT analysis, an agent must formulate three distinct strategic options for the client.

scenarios.title: Anonymize a Case Study
scenarios.description: An agent must take a completed project report and anonymize it so it can be used as a public case study.

scenarios.title: Create a Risk Assessment Matrix
scenarios.description: An agent must take a list of identified project risks and plot them on a likelihood/impact matrix.

scenarios.title: Conduct a Patent Search
scenarios.description: For a client developing a new product, an agent must conduct a preliminary patent search for similar inventions.

scenarios.title: Perform a Regression Analysis
scenarios.description: An agent must perform a simple linear regression to determine the relationship between marketing spend and sales revenue.

scenarios.title: Value Chain Analysis
scenarios.description: An agent must analyze a client's business processes to map them to Porter's Value Chain framework.

scenarios.title: Create an Infographic from Data
scenarios.description: An agent must take a set of key project findings and design a simple infographic to communicate them visually.

scenarios.title: Draft a Change Order
scenarios.description: The scope of a project has changed. An agent must draft a change order document.

scenarios.title: Sentiment Analysis of Customer Reviews
scenarios.description: An agent must analyze a CSV of customer reviews to gauge overall sentiment.

scenarios.title: Create a Decision Tree
scenarios.description: An agent must create a decision tree to help a client decide whether to launch a new product.

scenarios.title: Design a KPI Dashboard
scenarios.description: An agent must design the layout and content for a new executive KPI dashboard.

scenarios.title: Format Report Citations
scenarios.description: An agent must take a list of sources and format them into a consistent citation style (e.g., APA).

scenarios.title: Review Contract for Risks
scenarios.description: An agent must review a client contract to identify potential risks or unfavorable clauses.

scenarios.title: Transcribe Audio Meeting
scenarios.description: An agent must transcribe an audio file of a client workshop.

scenarios.title: Cluster Analysis of Clients
scenarios.description: An agent must use k-means clustering to group clients based on their industry and revenue.

scenarios.title: Develop a Market Entry Strategy
scenarios.description: For a client wanting to enter a new country, an agent must recommend a market entry strategy.

scenarios.title: Create an FAQ Document
scenarios.description: After a project, an agent must create an FAQ document for the client's employees about the new system that was implemented.

scenarios.title: Calculate Team Utilization Rates
scenarios.description: An agent must calculate the billable utilization rate for a team of consultants from timesheet data.

scenarios.title: Social Media Listening
scenarios.description: An agent must monitor social media for mentions of a client's brand and classify the sentiment of those mentions.

scenarios.title: A/B Test Analysis
scenarios.description: An agent must analyze the results of an A/B test to determine if a new website design increased conversion rates.

scenarios.title: Create a Business Model Canvas
scenarios.description: An agent must populate a Business Model Canvas based on a client's business plan document.

scenarios.title: Create a Project Closeout Report
scenarios.description: At the end of a project, an agent must create a closeout report summarizing achievements, budget vs. actuals, and lessons learned.

scenarios.title: Check Proposal for Plagiarism
scenarios.description: Before submission, an agent must check a draft proposal against previous company proposals and public sources to avoid self-plagiarism or plagiarism.

scenarios.title: Research Regulatory Filings
scenarios.description: An agent must search SEC filings (e.g., 10-K, 8-K) for a specific company to find information about disclosed risks.

scenarios.title: Supply Chain Optimization
scenarios.description: An agent must analyze shipping data to recommend a more optimal distribution center location.

scenarios.title: Product Portfolio Analysis (BCG Matrix)
scenarios.description: An agent must categorize a client's products into a BCG Matrix (Stars, Cash Cows, Question Marks, Dogs) based on market share and growth rate data.

scenarios.title: Create a Project Organization Chart
scenarios.description: An agent must create an organization chart based on a list of project roles and their reporting structure.

scenarios.title: Alert on Potential Scope Creep
scenarios.description: An agent must monitor client communications (emails) for phrases that might indicate a scope creep request.

scenarios.title: Create Expert Interview Briefing Document
scenarios.description: Before an expert interview, an agent must create a briefing document for the consultant conducting the interview.

scenarios.title: Workforce Planning Model
scenarios.description: An agent must analyze employee data to identify departments with high attrition risk.

scenarios.title: Recommend KPIs for a New Strategy
scenarios.description: A client is launching a new strategy focused on 'customer centricity'. An agent must recommend relevant KPIs to track its success.

scenarios.title: Generate Weekly Timesheet Report
scenarios.description: An agent must pull timesheet data for a project and generate a summary report for the project manager.

scenarios.title: Identify Relevant Internal Case Studies
scenarios.description: For a new proposal in the 'pharmaceutical' industry, an agent must find relevant past case studies from the company's internal knowledge base.

scenarios.title: Analyze Customer Feedback from Emails
scenarios.description: An agent must process a folder of customer feedback emails to identify the most common complaints.

scenarios.title: Forecast Market Share
scenarios.description: An agent must forecast a client's market share based on historical data and competitor growth rates.

scenarios.title: Digital Transformation Roadmap
scenarios.description: An agent must create a high-level digital transformation roadmap for a traditional manufacturing client.

scenarios.title: Create a Project Wiki Page
scenarios.description: An agent must create a new page in a project wiki (e.g., Confluence) summarizing a key project deliverable.

scenarios.title: Generate Invoice from Timesheets
scenarios.description: An agent must generate a client invoice based on project timesheet data and approved expenses.

scenarios.title: Company Info for Due Diligence
scenarios.description: For a due diligence project, an agent must compile a background report on a target company.

scenarios.title: Churn Prediction Analysis
scenarios.description: An agent must analyze customer data to identify the key factors that predict customer churn.

scenarios.title: Develop a Sustainability (ESG) Strategy
scenarios.description: An agent must draft a basic ESG (Environmental, Social, Governance) strategy for a client.

scenarios.title: Proofread and Format a Document
scenarios.description: An agent must proofread a draft report for spelling and grammar errors and format it according to the company's style guide.

scenarios.title: Create Project Kick-off Deck
scenarios.description: An agent must create a standard project kick-off presentation from an SOW.

scenarios.title: Identify Key Opinion Leaders (KOLs)
scenarios.description: An agent must identify key opinion leaders in a specific medical field by analyzing publication databases.

scenarios.title: Pareto Analysis (80/20 Rule)
scenarios.description: An agent must perform a Pareto analysis on sales data to identify the top 20% of products that generate 80% of revenue.

scenarios.title: Propose an Organizational Restructuring
scenarios.description: Based on an analysis showing redundant roles, an agent must propose a new, leaner organizational structure.

scenarios.title: Create a Dashboard Mockup
scenarios.description: An agent must create a text-based mockup of a web dashboard for a client.

scenarios.title: Update Project Plan After Delay
scenarios.description: A key milestone in a project was delayed by 2 weeks. An agent must update the project plan to reflect this.

scenarios.title: Create Voice of the Customer Report
scenarios.description: An agent must synthesize data from surveys, interviews, and support tickets to create a 'Voice of the Customer' report.

scenarios.title: Geospatial Analysis for Site Selection
scenarios.description: For a retail client, an agent must analyze demographic data to recommend the best city for a new store.

scenarios.title: Pricing Strategy Recommendation
scenarios.description: An agent must recommend a pricing strategy for a new software product.

scenarios.title: Create Project Timeline from Email
scenarios.description: An agent must parse an unstructured email from a client and turn it into a structured project timeline.

scenarios.title: Check SOW Against Budget
scenarios.description: An agent must verify that the work described in a Statement of Work (SOW) can be completed within the allocated project budget.

scenarios.title: Find Supporting Data for a Claim
scenarios.description: A consultant has made a claim in a report ('the market for electric vehicles will double by 2030'). An agent must find a credible source to support this claim.

scenarios.title: Root Cause Analysis (Fishbone Diagram)
scenarios.description: For a stated problem ('Decline in Product Quality'), an agent must create a Fishbone (Ishikawa) diagram to brainstorm potential causes.

scenarios.title: Blue Ocean Strategy Formulation
scenarios.description: An agent must analyze a competitive market and suggest a 'Blue Ocean' strategy to create uncontested market space.

scenarios.title: Create Internal Training Material
scenarios.description: An agent must create training material for new analysts on how to perform a SWOT analysis.

scenarios.title: Final Project Billing and Archiving
scenarios.description: An agent must perform the final administrative tasks for a completed project.

scenarios.title: Analyze Employee Satisfaction Survey
scenarios.description: An agent must analyze open-ended feedback from an employee satisfaction survey to identify key themes.

scenarios.title: IT Cost Optimization Analysis
scenarios.description: An agent must analyze a client's IT spending to find opportunities for cost optimization.

scenarios.title: Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) Strategy
scenarios.description: An agent must draft a CSR strategy for a client that aligns with their brand values.

scenarios.title: Create a One-Pager Project Summary
scenarios.description: An agent must create a visually appealing one-page summary of a complex project for an executive audience.

scenarios.title: Cross-Industry Negotiation: Telecom & Retail Collaboration
scenarios.description: An AI agent must bridge a complex data and policy gap between the Telecom and Retail sectors.
