scenarios.title: Promote a Story on Social Media
scenarios.description: A social media editor needs to promote a newly published investigative report across Twitter, Facebook, and Instagram.

scenarios.title: Moderate a Comments Section
scenarios.description: A community manager must moderate the comments section on a controversial article, removing hate speech while allowing for civil debate.

scenarios.title: Analyze Weekly Content Performance
scenarios.description: An editor wants to review the performance of all articles published last week to understand what resonated with the audience.

scenarios.title: Find an Old Article for a Follow-Up Story
scenarios.description: A reporter is writing a follow-up to a story published five years ago and needs to find the original article in the digital archive.

scenarios.title: Create a Newsletter Edition
scenarios.description: A newsletter editor needs to curate the top stories of the week and write a daily newsletter to be sent to subscribers.

scenarios.title: Respond to a Reader's Story Correction Request
scenarios.description: A reader has emailed to point out a factual error in a published article. An editor must investigate and, if necessary, issue a correction.

scenarios.title: Analyze Referral Traffic Sources
scenarios.description: A social media editor wants to know which platform (Twitter, Facebook, Reddit, etc.) is driving the most traffic to the website.

scenarios.title: Tag an Article for the Archive
scenarios.description: After a story is published, an archivist needs to add relevant metadata and tags to it before committing it to the long-term digital archive.

scenarios.title: Host a Q&A on Social Media
scenarios.description: Following a major investigation, the lead reporter hosts a live Q&A session on Twitter to answer audience questions.

scenarios.title: Analyze Audience Demographics
scenarios.description: An editor wants to understand the demographic makeup (age, gender, location) of their website's audience to better tailor content.

scenarios.title: Repurpose an Article as a Video Script
scenarios.description: A popular article is being turned into a short video. An editor needs to adapt the text into a script suitable for narration.

scenarios.title: Create a Social Video from an Article
scenarios.description: A social media editor needs to create a short, subtitled video for Facebook based on a recently published article.

scenarios.title: Run a Poll on Social Media
scenarios.description: To engage the audience around a current event, a social media editor decides to run a poll.

scenarios.title: Identify a Story Idea from Online Forums
scenarios.description: A community manager is monitoring a local online forum and notices many people are complaining about a specific local issue, suggesting a potential story.

scenarios.title: Analyze A/B Test Results for a Headline
scenarios.description: An A/B test was run on two different headlines for an article. An editor must analyze the results to see which performed better.

scenarios.title: Create a 'Best Of' Collection
scenarios.description: At the end of the year, an editor wants to create a collection of the publication's 10 best investigative reports from that year.

scenarios.title: Analyze Audience Engagement Funnel
scenarios.description: An analyst wants to see how many people who see a story on social media actually click the link, and then how long they stay on the page.

scenarios.title: Create a 'Story Highlights' for Instagram
scenarios.description: An editor wants to create a permanent 'Story Highlight' on the publication's Instagram profile for a major investigative series.

scenarios.title: Correct a Misleading Viral Rumor
scenarios.description: A false rumor about a public figure is spreading rapidly on social media. A journalist must quickly publish a story to debunk it.

scenarios.title: Analyze Reader Journey on the Website
scenarios.description: An analyst wants to understand the common paths readers take on the website. For example, what article do they often read after reading a story about the mayor?

scenarios.title: Find User-Generated Content During a Breaking News Event
scenarios.description: During a breaking news event, a social media editor searches for photos and videos posted by eyewitnesses on social media.

scenarios.title: Analyze 'Time of Day' Publication Performance
scenarios.description: An editor wants to know if articles published in the morning perform better than those published in the evening.

scenarios.title: Create a 'Behind the Scenes' Social Post
scenarios.description: To build a connection with the audience, a reporter posts a 'behind the scenes' photo or video from their reporting process.

scenarios.title: Manage a Live TV Call-In Segment
scenarios.description: A producer for a live TV news show must screen callers for a call-in segment with a political analyst.

scenarios.title: Draft a Breaking News Article
scenarios.description: A major, unexpected event has occurred. A reporter must quickly write a short, accurate article for immediate publication online.

scenarios.title: Copy Edit a Feature Story
scenarios.description: An editor needs to review a long-form feature story for grammar, style, clarity, and adherence to the publication's style guide.

scenarios.title: Create a Video Package from an Interview
scenarios.description: A video journalist has a 30-minute interview and needs to edit it down to a 2-minute video package for the web, adding B-roll and graphics.

scenarios.title: Publish an Article to the CMS
scenarios.description: An online editor needs to take a finalized article and publish it to the website using the content management system (CMS).

scenarios.title: Write a Human-Interest Feature
scenarios.description: A reporter needs to write a long-form feature story about a local hero, focusing on narrative and character development.

scenarios.title: Check a Story for Plagiarism
scenarios.description: Before publication, an editor must check a freelance submission to ensure it is original work and does not contain plagiarism.

scenarios.title: Create an Infographic from Data
scenarios.description: A data journalist has a spreadsheet of complex data and needs to create a simple, clear infographic to help readers understand the key findings.

scenarios.title: Write Alternative Headlines for A/B Testing
scenarios.description: An editor wants to test which headline drives more traffic to an article and needs to write several different options.

scenarios.title: Write an Obituary
scenarios.description: A prominent community member has passed away. A reporter must write a respectful and comprehensive obituary.

scenarios.title: Review a Restaurant
scenarios.description: A food critic has visited a new restaurant and needs to write a review, balancing description of the food, atmosphere, and service.

scenarios.title: Select Photos for a Photo Essay
scenarios.description: A photographer has submitted 200 photos from an event. A photo editor must select the 10-15 best images that tell a cohesive story.

scenarios.title: Update a Live Blog
scenarios.description: During a developing news event (like an election night), an editor must continuously update a live blog with the latest information from reporters.

scenarios.title: Write a Script for a Podcast
scenarios.description: A podcast host needs to write a script for an upcoming episode that includes an introduction, interview questions, and an outro.

scenarios.title: Write an Explainer Article
scenarios.description: A complex topic is in the news (e.g., a new piece of legislation). A reporter needs to write an 'explainer' article that breaks it down for a general audience.

scenarios.title: Headline and Summary for an External Wire Story
scenarios.description: An editor needs to take a story from a wire service (like AP or Reuters), write a new headline and summary for it, and publish it on their own site.

scenarios.title: Create a Photo Gallery for a Website
scenarios.description: A photo editor needs to create a web-based photo gallery from a recent event.

scenarios.title: Schedule a Story for Later Publication
scenarios.description: A story is finished and edited, but the editor wants to publish it next morning during peak traffic hours.

scenarios.title: Edit Audio for a Podcast
scenarios.description: A podcast producer needs to edit a recorded interview, removing mistakes, long pauses, and 'ums' and 'ahs'.

scenarios.title: Choose a 'Pull Quote' for an Article Layout
scenarios.description: A print designer needs a 'pull quote' (a short, compelling excerpt) from a feature story to use as a large, graphical element in the layout.

scenarios.title: Write a TV News Script
scenarios.description: A broadcast journalist needs to write a 90-second script for the evening news about a city council meeting.

scenarios.title: Fact-Check a Reporter's Draft
scenarios.description: An editor or dedicated fact-checker must review a reporter's completed draft to verify every factual statement.

scenarios.title: Write a Weather Forecast Script
scenarios.description: A TV meteorologist needs to write a script for their weather segment.

scenarios.title: Create a 'Timeline' Graphic
scenarios.description: For a story that unfolds over a long period, a visuals editor needs to create a timeline graphic to help readers understand the sequence of events.

scenarios.title: Write a Film Review
scenarios.description: A film critic needs to write a review of a new movie.

scenarios.title: Write SEO-Friendly Article
scenarios.description: A reporter is writing an article on a popular topic and needs to ensure it is optimized to rank well in search engine results.

scenarios.title: Write a Year-in-Review Article
scenarios.description: An editor needs to write a major feature article summarizing the most important news events of the past year.

scenarios.title: Assign a Breaking News Story
scenarios.description: An editor needs to assign a breaking news story to the most appropriate available reporter.

scenarios.title: Conduct a Legal Review of an Investigative Piece
scenarios.description: Before publishing a sensitive investigative story that names individuals, an editor must have it reviewed by the legal department for libel risk.

scenarios.title: Collaborate on a Data-Driven Story
scenarios.description: A reporter and a data journalist are collaborating on a story. They need a shared space to work on the data, analysis, and draft.

scenarios.title: Book Travel for an Overseas Assignment
scenarios.description: A reporter is being sent overseas to cover a conflict and needs flights, a hotel, and a local fixer booked.

scenarios.title: Pitch a Story Idea at an Editorial Meeting
scenarios.description: A reporter has an idea for a long-form investigative piece and needs to pitch it to their editors to get it approved.

scenarios.title: Check if a Photo is Cleared for Use
scenarios.description: An editor wants to use a photo found online, but must first verify that the publication has the legal right to use it.

scenarios.title: Plan Coverage for a Major Scheduled Event
scenarios.description: Editors are planning coverage for the upcoming Olympics. They need to coordinate assignments for multiple reporters and photographers.

scenarios.title: Request Camera Equipment from Gear Closet
scenarios.description: A video journalist needs to check out a specific camera, lens, and microphone for an upcoming shoot.

scenarios.title: Check Story Against the Day's News Budget
scenarios.description: An editor reviews the list of stories planned for the day (the 'news budget') to ensure there is a good mix of topics and no overlap.

scenarios.title: Handle a Source's Request to be Anonymous
scenarios.description: A source providing sensitive information will only speak if they are granted anonymity. The reporter must get an editor's approval.

scenarios.title: Share Files with a Freelancer
scenarios.description: An editor needs to securely share background documents and interview transcripts with a freelance reporter who is not on the internal network.

scenarios.title: File an Expense Report
scenarios.description: A reporter has returned from an assignment and needs to file an expense report to be reimbursed for travel and food costs.

scenarios.title: Track a Reporter's Progress on a Story
scenarios.description: An editor needs to check in on a reporter's progress for a long-term story to ensure it is on track to meet its deadline.

scenarios.title: Anonymize a Source in a Draft
scenarios.description: A story uses an anonymous source. An editor must ensure the draft does not contain any details that could inadvertently identify the person.

scenarios.title: Set up a Team Meeting
scenarios.description: An editor needs to schedule a meeting with three reporters and a photo editor to plan upcoming coverage.

scenarios.title: Arrange for a Press Pass
scenarios.description: A reporter needs a press pass to gain access to a secure event, like a political convention.

scenarios.title: Decline a Story Pitch
scenarios.description: A reporter pitches a story, but the editor decides it is not a good fit for the publication and must decline it constructively.

scenarios.title: Handle a Subpoena for a Reporter's Notes
scenarios.description: The news organization has received a subpoena from a court demanding a reporter's notes for a story. This must be handled by the legal department.

scenarios.title: Coordinate Between a Reporter and a Photographer
scenarios.description: An editor needs to make sure a reporter and a photographer, who are in different locations, meet up at the right time and place for an assignment.

scenarios.title: Check for Conflict of Interest
scenarios.description: A reporter has been assigned a story about a company. The editor must check if the reporter has any financial or personal conflicts of interest.

scenarios.title: Archive a Reporter's Notes
scenarios.description: After a major investigation is published, the reporter's notes and source documents must be securely archived for legal and historical purposes.

scenarios.title: Handle an Angry Call from a Story Subject
scenarios.description: A person who was the subject of a critical story calls the newsroom, angry about their portrayal. An editor must handle the call.

scenarios.title: Update the Newsroom's Style Guide
scenarios.description: A new term has entered the public lexicon. A senior editor must decide on the correct spelling and usage and add it to the internal style guide.

scenarios.title: Plan for a Reporter's Parental Leave
scenarios.description: A reporter is going on parental leave for three months. Their editor must plan to reassign their beat and ongoing stories.

scenarios.title: Researching a Company for an Exposé
scenarios.description: A reporter is starting an investigation into a local company suspected of environmental violations and needs to gather initial background information.

scenarios.title: Find an Expert Source for a Science Story
scenarios.description: A reporter is writing about a new medical breakthrough and needs to find a qualified, independent expert to comment on the research.

scenarios.title: File a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) Request
scenarios.description: A journalist needs to obtain government records about a public contract and must file a formal FOIA request.

scenarios.title: Cover a Live Press Conference
scenarios.description: A reporter is covering a live press conference with the mayor and needs to capture key quotes and ask a relevant question.

scenarios.title: Transcribe a Recorded Interview
scenarios.description: A reporter has a one-hour audio recording of an interview and needs a written transcript to pull quotes from.

scenarios.title: Fact-Check a Politician's Speech
scenarios.description: A politician has just given a major speech. A fact-checker needs to verify the claims and statistics used in the speech.

scenarios.title: Cover a Court Trial
scenarios.description: A reporter is assigned to cover a high-profile criminal trial and must provide daily updates.

scenarios.title: Develop a Story from a Press Release
scenarios.description: A reporter receives a press release about a new product. They must go beyond the release to write an original story.

scenarios.title: Verify an Anonymous Tip
scenarios.description: A reporter receives an anonymous tip about corruption at a government agency. They must try to verify the information before proceeding.

scenarios.title: Schedule an Interview with a Hostile Source
scenarios.description: A reporter needs to interview a public figure who is known to be hostile to the press.

scenarios.title: Analyze Campaign Finance Data
scenarios.description: A political reporter has a large dataset of campaign contributions and needs to find patterns, such as which industries are donating the most to a candidate.

scenarios.title: Live-Tweet a Sporting Event
scenarios.description: A sports reporter is covering a championship game and needs to provide real-time updates and commentary on Twitter.

scenarios.title: Scrape Data from a Government Website
scenarios.description: A data journalist needs to collect data from a government website that does not offer a downloadable file. They must write a script to scrape the data.

scenarios.title: Background a Political Candidate
scenarios.description: A political reporter is assigned to cover a new candidate running for office and must research their entire public history.

scenarios.title: Conduct an Off-the-Record Briefing
scenarios.description: A source is willing to provide background information to help a reporter understand a complex topic, but does not want to be quoted. The reporter must conduct an 'off-the-record' briefing.

scenarios.title: Verify a Viral Social Media Video
scenarios.description: A dramatic video of an event is going viral. A reporter must verify its authenticity before reporting on it.

scenarios.title: Report from a Natural Disaster Zone
scenarios.description: A reporter is sent to cover a hurricane's aftermath. They must report on the damage and the human impact while dealing with difficult conditions.

scenarios.title: Find a Case Study for a Trend Story
scenarios.description: A reporter is writing about a national economic trend (e.g., inflation) and needs to find a local person or family to serve as a case study.

scenarios.title: Prepare for an Interview
scenarios.description: A reporter is about to conduct a major interview and needs to prepare a thorough list of questions and background materials.

scenarios.title: Cover an Election Night
scenarios.description: A political reporter is responsible for covering election results as they come in.

scenarios.title: Find a Person's Contact Information
scenarios.description: A reporter needs to find a phone number or email for a private citizen they want to interview.

scenarios.title: Follow a Money Trail
scenarios.description: A reporter is investigating a politician and wants to follow a money trail from a government contract to the politician's personal finances.

scenarios.title: Cover a Protest
scenarios.description: A reporter is sent to cover a protest. They need to report on the size of the crowd, their message, and any interactions with counter-protesters or police.

scenarios.title: Develop Story Idea from Public Data
scenarios.description: A data journalist browses a city's open data portal looking for potential stories.

scenarios.title: Verify Authenticity of a Leaked Document
scenarios.description: A reporter receives a supposedly leaked document. They must take steps to verify it is real before reporting on it.

scenarios.title: Go Undercover for a Story
scenarios.description: For a major investigation, a reporter needs to go undercover (e.g., get a job at a company) to expose wrongdoing. This requires extensive planning and ethical review.

scenarios.title: Initial Case Assessment
scenarios.description: A senior partner needs to assess a new potential litigation matter to determine its viability and strategy.

scenarios.title: Review a Standard Vendor Agreement
scenarios.description: An in-house counsel needs to review a standard, third-party vendor agreement for potential risks.

scenarios.title: Track and Record Billable Hours
scenarios.description: An associate attorney needs to record their billable hours for the day for multiple matters.

scenarios.title: Conduct Document Review for Relevance
scenarios.description: A paralegal is assigned a batch of 1,000 documents in an e-discovery platform and must code them for relevance to the case.

scenarios.title: Draft a Standard Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA)
scenarios.description: A corporate attorney needs to draft a standard, one-way NDA for a potential business partnership.

scenarios.title: Run a Conflicts Check
scenarios.description: Before taking on a new client, a paralegal must run a conflicts check to ensure the firm can ethically represent them.

scenarios.title: Draft a Motion for Summary Judgment
scenarios.description: A litigation associate needs to draft a motion for summary judgment, arguing that there are no genuine disputes of material fact.

scenarios.title: Conduct Legal Due Diligence
scenarios.description: As part of an M&A transaction, a junior associate needs to review a target company's key contracts to identify any legal risks.

scenarios.title: Send Case Status Update to Client
scenarios.description: A partner asks a paralegal to send a status update to a client on a litigation matter.

scenarios.title: Research a Specific Legal Question
scenarios.description: An associate needs to research case law to answer a specific question: 'What constitutes 'reasonable accommodation' for a religious practice under Title VII in the 9th Circuit?'

scenarios.title: File a Trademark Application
scenarios.description: An IP paralegal needs to file a new trademark application with the USPTO for a client's new product name.

scenarios.title: Calendar Court-Ordered Deadlines
scenarios.description: A paralegal receives a scheduling order from a court and must calendar all deadlines.

scenarios.title: Prepare a Privilege Log
scenarios.description: After a document review, a litigation paralegal needs to create a privilege log for all documents that were withheld from production based on attorney-client privilege.

scenarios.title: Draft Board Meeting Minutes
scenarios.description: A corporate paralegal needs to draft the official minutes for a recent board of directors meeting.

scenarios.title: Generate a Client Invoice
scenarios.description: At the end of the month, a billing coordinator needs to generate an invoice for a client.

scenarios.title: File a Complaint with the Court
scenarios.description: A paralegal needs to electronically file a new complaint with a federal court.

scenarios.title: Execute a Contract via E-signature
scenarios.description: After negotiations are complete, an attorney needs to send a final version of a contract to both parties for electronic signature.

scenarios.title: Check for New Ethics Opinions
scenarios.description: A law firm's ethics counsel needs to check for any new ethics opinions issued by the state bar association in the last month.

scenarios.title: Prepare a Deposition Summary
scenarios.description: A junior associate needs to read a 200-page deposition transcript and prepare a summary for the lead attorney.

scenarios.title: Update a Corporate Minute Book
scenarios.description: Following an annual shareholder meeting, a corporate paralegal needs to update the official corporate minute book.

scenarios.title: Provide Case Update to Inquiring Client
scenarios.description: A client calls asking for a quick update on their case. The assigned paralegal needs to provide a concise and accurate status.

scenarios.title: Draft Responses to Interrogatories
scenarios.description: A litigation associate must draft responses to a set of written interrogatories (questions) served by the opposing party.

scenarios.title: Manage an IP Portfolio
scenarios.description: An IP paralegal needs to review a client's trademark portfolio to identify upcoming renewal deadlines.

scenarios.title: Calculate a Statute of Limitations
scenarios.description: For a new personal injury case, a paralegal must calculate the statute of limitations to determine the deadline for filing a lawsuit.

scenarios.title: Draft a Cease and Desist Letter
scenarios.description: An attorney needs to draft a cease and desist letter to a third party who is infringing on a client's trademark.

scenarios.title: Negotiate a Contract Clause
scenarios.description: During a contract negotiation, an in-house counsel needs to redline a clause and propose alternative language.

scenarios.title: Handle a Client Complaint about Billing
scenarios.description: A client has called to complain about the amount of their latest invoice, questioning the time spent on a task.

scenarios.title: Draft an Answer to a Complaint
scenarios.description: A client has been sued, and a litigation associate needs to draft an answer to the complaint.

scenarios.title: Prepare a Closing Binder for an M&A Deal
scenarios.description: After an M&A deal has closed, a corporate paralegal needs to assemble a 'closing binder' with all the final, executed deal documents.

scenarios.title: Close Out a Matter
scenarios.description: A case has concluded, and a paralegal needs to perform the administrative steps to close the matter.

scenarios.title: Redact a Document for Production
scenarios.description: During discovery, an attorney needs to redact privileged information from a document before producing it to the other side.

scenarios.title: Draft an IP Licensing Agreement
scenarios.description: A corporate attorney needs to draft an agreement to license the company's patented technology to a third party.

scenarios.title: Review Client Billing Guidelines
scenarios.description: Before starting work on a new matter for a large corporate client, a paralegal must review the client's specific billing guidelines to ensure compliance.

scenarios.title: Shepardize a Case
scenarios.description: Before citing a case in a brief, an associate must Shepardize it (or use a similar tool like KeyCite) to ensure it is still good law.

scenarios.title: Form a New LLC
scenarios.description: A corporate paralegal needs to form a new Limited Liability Company (LLC) for a client in the state of Delaware.

scenarios.title: Request an Extension of Time
scenarios.description: An attorney needs to request a 14-day extension from opposing counsel to respond to a set of discovery requests.

scenarios.title: Prepare a Witness for Deposition
scenarios.description: An attorney needs to prepare a key witness for an upcoming deposition.

scenarios.title: Analyze Contract for Termination Clause
scenarios.description: A client wants to terminate a contract, and an attorney needs to analyze the agreement to understand their rights and any penalties.

scenarios.title: Process a Trust Account Transaction
scenarios.description: A firm receives a settlement check on behalf of a client, which must be deposited into the client's trust account.

scenarios.title: Serve a Subpoena
scenarios.description: A paralegal needs to arrange for a third-party witness to be served with a deposition subpoena.

scenarios.title: Draft an Employment Agreement
scenarios.description: An in-house counsel needs to draft a standard employment agreement for a new executive.

scenarios.title: Issue a Litigation Hold Notice
scenarios.description: When new litigation is anticipated, an in-house counsel must issue a litigation hold notice to all relevant employees to preserve documents.

scenarios.title: Cite-Check a Legal Brief
scenarios.description: A junior paralegal must cite-check a brief before it is filed, ensuring all case citations are in the correct format (e.g., Bluebook) and accurately state the cited proposition.

scenarios.title: Draft a Corporate Resolution
scenarios.description: A company needs to open a new bank account, which requires a formal corporate resolution. A paralegal needs to draft it.

scenarios.title: Process an Expense Against a Matter
scenarios.description: An attorney incurred an expense (e.g., a filing fee) and needs to have it recorded against the correct client matter for billing.

scenarios.title: Prepare Exhibits for a Motion
scenarios.description: A paralegal needs to prepare and organize the exhibits that will be filed in support of a motion.

scenarios.title: Track a Contract Renewal Date
scenarios.description: An in-house legal department needs to track the renewal date of a key software license to decide whether to renew or terminate.

scenarios.title: Handle a New Client Intake Call
scenarios.description: A potential client calls the firm for the first time. The intake specialist needs to gather initial information.

scenarios.title: Search for Patent Prior Art
scenarios.description: Before filing a patent application, a patent agent needs to search for 'prior art' to assess the novelty of an invention.

scenarios.title: Check Local Court Rules
scenarios.description: Before filing a motion, a paralegal must check the local court rules and the specific judge's rules for any special formatting or filing requirements.

scenarios.title: Create an Ethical Wall
scenarios.description: A firm takes on a new matter that is adverse to a former client. To avoid a conflict, the firm needs to create an 'ethical wall' to screen off the attorneys who worked on the former matter.

scenarios.title: Summarize a Deposition Transcript
scenarios.description: A junior lawyer needs to create a concise summary of a key witness's deposition testimony.

scenarios.title: Create a Virtual Data Room for M&A
scenarios.description: In an M&A deal, the seller's counsel needs to set up a virtual data room (VDR) for the buyer to conduct due diligence.

scenarios.title: Check on Case Appeal Status
scenarios.description: A client wants to know the status of their appeal. A paralegal needs to check the appellate court's docket.

scenarios.title: Produce Documents in Discovery
scenarios.description: After completing a document review, a paralegal needs to produce the 'Responsive' and non-privileged documents to the opposing counsel.

scenarios.title: Draft a Website Privacy Policy
scenarios.description: An in-house counsel needs to draft a new privacy policy for the company's website to comply with regulations like GDPR and CCPA.

scenarios.title: Review and Approve a Vendor Invoice
scenarios.description: A managing partner needs to review and approve an invoice from an expert witness before it can be paid.

scenarios.title: Prepare for Oral Argument
scenarios.description: An attorney is preparing for an oral argument before an appellate court and needs to anticipate potential questions from the judges.

scenarios.title: Manage Contract Versions
scenarios.description: During a lengthy negotiation, multiple versions of a contract are being exchanged. A paralegal needs to manage version control.

scenarios.title: Clear a Trademark for Use
scenarios.description: A client wants to use a new name for a product. An IP attorney needs to conduct a full trademark search to assess the risk of infringement.

scenarios.title: Draft an Affidavit
scenarios.description: In support of a motion, an attorney needs to draft an affidavit for a witness to sign, attesting to certain facts.

scenarios.title: Onboard a New Attorney
scenarios.description: A new attorney is joining the firm. The practice manager needs to set them up in all firm systems.

scenarios.title: Respond to a Third-Party Subpoena
scenarios.description: A company (not a party to the lawsuit) receives a subpoena for documents. In-house counsel must manage the response.

scenarios.title: Create a Contract Summary
scenarios.description: A business executive needs a one-page summary of a long, complex contract, highlighting the key business terms.

scenarios.title: Request Documents from a Client
scenarios.description: To respond to discovery, a paralegal needs to request a specific set of documents from the client.

scenarios.title: Analyze Opponent's Brief for Key Arguments
scenarios.description: An attorney needs to analyze an opponent's motion brief to identify their key legal arguments in order to draft a response.

scenarios.title: File a Corporate Annual Report
scenarios.description: A corporate paralegal needs to file a company's annual report with the Secretary of State to keep it in good standing.

scenarios.title: Update Case Calendar with New Date
scenarios.description: A deposition has been rescheduled. A paralegal needs to update the case calendar.

scenarios.title: Prepare for a 'Meet and Confer'
scenarios.description: Before filing a motion to compel, attorneys are required to 'meet and confer' to resolve a discovery dispute. An associate needs to prepare for this meeting.

scenarios.title: Draft an M&A Deal Announcement
scenarios.description: After an M&A deal is signed, an in-house counsel needs to draft an internal announcement to all employees.

scenarios.title: Handle a Client Billing Inquiry
scenarios.description: A client has a question about a specific line item on their invoice. A billing coordinator needs to provide an explanation.

scenarios.title: File a Document Under Seal
scenarios.description: An attorney needs to file a document that contains highly confidential trade secrets, requiring it to be filed under seal.

scenarios.title: Respond to a Trademark Office Action
scenarios.description: The USPTO has issued an Office Action refusing a trademark application. An IP attorney needs to draft a response.

scenarios.title: Run Final Conflicts Check Before Filing
scenarios.description: Just before filing a new lawsuit, a partner asks for a final conflicts check to be run, including newly added defendants.

scenarios.title: Bates Stamp Documents for Production
scenarios.description: A paralegal needs to apply unique Bates stamp numbers to a set of documents before producing them in discovery.

scenarios.title: Create a Closing Checklist for an M&A Deal
scenarios.description: A corporate associate needs to create a closing checklist that details all the documents that need to be signed and actions that need to be taken to close a deal.

scenarios.title: Send an Engagement Letter to a New Client
scenarios.description: After a new client agrees to retain the firm, a paralegal needs to send them the official engagement letter for signature.

scenarios.title: Draft a Stipulated Protective Order
scenarios.description: In a case involving confidential information, the parties agree to a stipulated protective order to govern the handling of sensitive documents. An associate needs to draft it.

scenarios.title: Review Corporate Bylaws
scenarios.description: A client is unsure about the process for electing a new director. In-house counsel needs to review the corporate bylaws to provide an answer.

scenarios.title: Perform a Lien Search
scenarios.description: As part of a real estate transaction, a paralegal needs to perform a search to see if there are any outstanding liens on the property.

scenarios.title: Request Medical Records
scenarios.description: In a personal injury case, a paralegal needs to request the plaintiff's medical records from a hospital.

scenarios.title: Write Off an Uncollectible Invoice
scenarios.description: After numerous attempts to collect, a firm decides to write off an old, unpaid invoice. The billing manager needs to process this.

scenarios.title: Summarize New Legislation
scenarios.description: A new law has been passed that affects a client's industry. An associate needs to summarize the law and its impact for the client.

scenarios.title: Draft a Settlement Agreement
scenarios.description: The parties in a lawsuit have reached a settlement. An attorney needs to draft the settlement agreement to formalize the terms.

scenarios.title: Send Case Files to Co-Counsel
scenarios.description: A firm is bringing on co-counsel for a large case and needs to share the primary case files with them.

scenarios.title: File a Notice of Appearance
scenarios.description: An attorney is taking over a case from a prior counsel and needs to file a notice of appearance with the court.

scenarios.title: Create a Contract Abstraction
scenarios.description: For a large portfolio of real estate leases, a paralegal needs to create 'abstractions' or summaries of each lease, extracting key data into a spreadsheet.

scenarios.title: Check Attorney CLE Compliance
scenarios.description: A firm administrator needs to check the Continuing Legal Education (CLE) compliance status for all attorneys in the firm ahead of the annual reporting deadline.

scenarios.title: Create a Closing Argument Outline
scenarios.description: An attorney is preparing for trial and needs to create an outline for their closing argument.

scenarios.title: Handle a Data Breach Response
scenarios.description: A company has experienced a data breach. In-house counsel must coordinate the legal response.

scenarios.title: Create a Case Budget
scenarios.description: A client has requested a budget for a new litigation matter. A senior associate needs to create a phased budget.

scenarios.title: Organize Discovery Documents
scenarios.description: A firm has received a large production of documents from the opposing party. A paralegal needs to organize them.

scenarios.title: Create Signature Packets for a Closing
scenarios.description: For a large real estate closing, a paralegal needs to prepare signature packets for each signatory with all the documents they need to sign.

scenarios.title: Update Attorney Bio on Firm Website
scenarios.description: An attorney has been promoted to partner. The marketing department needs to update their bio on the firm's website.

scenarios.title: Draft a Demand Letter for Unpaid Invoice
scenarios.description: A client has not been paid by a customer. An attorney needs to draft a demand letter before filing a lawsuit.

scenarios.title: File a UCC Financing Statement
scenarios.description: In a secured lending transaction, the lender's counsel needs to file a UCC-1 financing statement to perfect the lender's security interest in the collateral.

scenarios.title: Review Outside Counsel Bills
scenarios.description: An in-house counsel needs to review invoices from an outside law firm for compliance with the company's billing guidelines.

scenarios.title: Create a Trial Exhibit List
scenarios.description: In preparation for trial, a paralegal needs to create a list of all exhibits the party intends to use.

scenarios.title: Draft Website Terms of Service
scenarios.description: An in-house counsel needs to draft the Terms of Service (ToS) for a new software-as-a-service (SaaS) product.

scenarios.title: Close Out an Ethics Review
scenarios.description: After a conflicts check revealed a potential issue, the firm's ethics committee reviewed it and cleared the firm to take the case. An administrator needs to document this.

scenarios.title: Perform a Cycle Count
scenarios.description: An inventory controller is assigned a set of locations to count to ensure inventory accuracy.

scenarios.title: Process a Return to Stock
scenarios.description: A returned item has been inspected and deemed resalable. An operator must return it to inventory.

scenarios.title: Identify Slow-Moving Inventory
scenarios.description: An inventory analyst needs to run a report to identify products that have not sold in the last 180 days.

scenarios.title: Create a Stock Transfer Order
scenarios.description: A distribution center (DC A) is low on stock for a popular item. An inventory planner needs to create a stock transfer order to move inventory from another DC (DC B).

scenarios.title: Investigate Inventory Discrepancy
scenarios.description: A picker reports a short pick because an item was not in its primary location. An inventory controller must investigate why.

scenarios.title: Process Scrapped Inventory
scenarios.description: An item returned by a customer is deemed unsalable and must be scrapped.

scenarios.title: Place a Purchase Order
scenarios.description: An item's inventory has fallen below its reorder point. A buyer needs to place a purchase order with the supplier.

scenarios.title: Forecast Seasonal Demand
scenarios.description: An inventory analyst needs to forecast demand for a seasonal item (e.g., winter coats) to inform purchasing decisions.

scenarios.title: Quarantine a Recalled Product
scenarios.description: A supplier has issued a recall for a specific batch of a product. An inventory manager must find all units from that batch and place them on hold.

scenarios.title: Process a Supplier Credit Memo for Shortage
scenarios.description: A shipment was received with a shortage. The supplier has sent a credit memo for the missing items, which needs to be applied.

scenarios.title: Manage Expiring Inventory (FEFO)
scenarios.description: In a food-grade warehouse, an operator picking an order must be directed to pick the batch with the earliest expiration date first.

scenarios.title: Perform Blind Receiving
scenarios.description: To ensure accuracy, a warehouse is configured for 'blind receiving', where operators don't see the expected PO quantity and must count from scratch.

scenarios.title: Create a Virtual Kit (Bill of Materials)
scenarios.description: A marketing team wants to sell a new product bundle online. An inventory analyst must create a virtual kit in the system so that when the kit is sold, the system allocates the component inventory.

scenarios.title: Adjust Inventory for Warehouse Damage
scenarios.description: A forklift operator accidentally damages a pallet of goods in the warehouse. The damaged items must be adjusted out of inventory.

scenarios.title: Process a Return for Refurbishment
scenarios.description: A returned electronic item is not resalable as-is but can be refurbished. It needs to be routed to the refurbishment department.

scenarios.title: Resolve Negative Inventory
scenarios.description: A system report shows a negative on-hand quantity for an item, which is physically impossible. An inventory controller must investigate and correct it.

scenarios.title: Set a Reorder Point for a New Item
scenarios.description: A new product is being introduced. An inventory planner must set its initial reorder point and safety stock levels in the system.

scenarios.title: Analyze Inventory Turnover
scenarios.description: An analyst wants to calculate the inventory turnover rate for a specific product category to assess its efficiency.

scenarios.title: Receive a Return Without an RMA
scenarios.description: A customer sends back a return without first getting a Return Merchandise Authorization (RMA). The returns department must handle this exception.

scenarios.title: Create an Inventory Hold for Quality Inspection
scenarios.description: A batch of newly received items must be held in a quarantine status until the quality assurance team can inspect and release it.

scenarios.title: Reconcile Annual Physical Inventory
scenarios.description: After a full wall-to-wall physical inventory count, an accountant must reconcile the physical counts with the system records and post the financial adjustment.

scenarios.title: Identify Inventory Ownership (3PL)
scenarios.description: In a multi-client 3PL warehouse, an operator needs to know which client owns the inventory in a specific location.

scenarios.title: Perform ABC Inventory Analysis
scenarios.description: An analyst wants to classify all products into A, B, or C categories based on their value and sales volume to prioritize inventory management efforts.

scenarios.title: Receive a Shipment with an ASN
scenarios.description: A shipment arrives from a supplier who sent an Advance Ship Notice (ASN). The receiving process should be faster as the details are already in the system.

scenarios.title: Dispose of Obsolete Inventory
scenarios.description: A product has been discontinued and the remaining stock is deemed obsolete. It needs to be disposed of.

scenarios.title: Process a Customer Return (RMA)
scenarios.description: A customer service agent needs to process a return request for a customer who received a damaged item.

scenarios.title: Handle Shipping Address Change Request
scenarios.description: A customer calls to change the shipping address for an order that has not yet been shipped.

scenarios.title: Allocate Inventory to a High-Priority Order
scenarios.description: A high-priority sales order comes in, and an agent must manually allocate available inventory to it before it's released to the warehouse.

scenarios.title: Generate a Bill of Lading (BOL)
scenarios.description: A shipment is ready to be picked up by a carrier. The shipping clerk must generate the legal Bill of Lading document.

scenarios.title: Cancel an Order Before Shipping
scenarios.description: A customer requests to cancel an order that has been processed but not yet shipped.

scenarios.title: Generate Commercial Invoice for International Shipment
scenarios.description: An international order is being shipped, and it requires a commercial invoice for customs purposes.

scenarios.title: Handle a Lost Shipment Inquiry
scenarios.description: A customer calls because their tracking information has not updated in several days, and they believe the shipment is lost.

scenarios.title: Process an EDI 850 Purchase Order
scenarios.description: An EDI 850 (Purchase Order) document is received from a major retail partner and must be automatically processed.

scenarios.title: Provide a Shipping Quote
scenarios.description: A sales agent is on the phone with a potential customer and needs to provide a real-time shipping quote for an order.

scenarios.title: Handle a Backorder Situation
scenarios.description: A customer places an order for an item that is out of stock. The agent must inform the customer and provide options.

scenarios.title: Provide Order Status Update to Customer
scenarios.description: A customer contacts support to ask for the status of their recent order.

scenarios.title: Split an Order for Partial Shipment
scenarios.description: An order contains two items, but one is on backorder. The customer wants the in-stock item to ship now.

scenarios.title: Handle a Damaged Shipment Claim
scenarios.description: A customer reports that their order arrived damaged. The agent must process the claim and send a replacement.

scenarios.title: Generate a Packing List
scenarios.description: Before an order is packed, the system must generate a packing list to be included inside the box.

scenarios.title: Send Advance Ship Notice (ASN)
scenarios.description: After a shipment for a major retail partner has been dispatched, an EDI 856 (Advance Ship Notice) must be sent.

scenarios.title: Handle an Order with High Fraud Risk
scenarios.description: An online order is automatically flagged by the system as having a high risk of fraud (e.g., mismatched billing/shipping addresses, proxy IP).

scenarios.title: Generate Customer Performance Report
scenarios.description: An account manager needs to generate a quarterly business review (QBR) report for a key client, showing metrics like on-time shipping and order accuracy.

scenarios.title: Process a Drop-Ship Order
scenarios.description: A customer orders an item that is not stocked in the warehouse but is instead shipped directly from a supplier (drop-shipped).

scenarios.title: Handle 'Wrong Item Shipped' Claim
scenarios.description: A customer reports they received the wrong item. The agent must arrange for the return of the wrong item and shipment of the correct one.

scenarios.title: Report on Order Fill Rate
scenarios.description: A manager wants to analyze the order fill rate (the percentage of orders shipped complete without backorders) for the previous month.

scenarios.title: Handle a Short Shipment Claim
scenarios.description: A customer claims they were shorted an item in their shipment. The agent must investigate and resolve.

scenarios.title: Route Order to Optimal DC
scenarios.description: An e-commerce order is placed. The system must decide which distribution center (DC) to fulfill it from to minimize shipping cost and time.

scenarios.title: Validate a Shipping Address
scenarios.description: When a customer enters their address during online checkout, the system must validate it to ensure it's a deliverable address and to prevent shipping errors.

scenarios.title: Handle an Order with Special Instructions
scenarios.description: A customer adds a note to their order: 'Please gift wrap'. This instruction must be visible to the warehouse packer.

scenarios.title: Track In-Transit Shipment for Customer
scenarios.description: A customer service agent receives a call asking for the status of an in-transit shipment. The agent must locate the shipment and provide an ETA.

scenarios.title: Tender a Full Truckload (FTL) Shipment
scenarios.description: A logistics coordinator needs to find a carrier for a full truckload shipment and tender the load.

scenarios.title: File Freight Claim for Damage
scenarios.description: A shipment was delivered with visible damage. A claims specialist must file a claim with the carrier.

scenarios.title: Audit a Freight Invoice
scenarios.description: An accounts payable clerk needs to audit a carrier's freight invoice against the agreed-upon rate before approving it for payment.

scenarios.title: Schedule a Delivery Appointment
scenarios.description: A shipment is approaching its destination, and the carrier needs to schedule a delivery appointment with the receiving facility.

scenarios.title: Manage Carrier On-Time Performance
scenarios.description: A carrier manager needs to review the on-time pickup and delivery performance for a specific carrier over the last quarter.

scenarios.title: Handle a Delivery Refusal
scenarios.description: A carrier reports that a customer has refused a delivery. The agent must determine the next steps.

scenarios.title: Capture Proof of Delivery (POD)
scenarios.description: A carrier's driver has completed a delivery and needs to upload the signed Bill of Lading as Proof of Delivery.

scenarios.title: Consolidate LTL Shipments into a Multi-Stop FTL
scenarios.description: A planner has several LTL shipments going to the same region and wants to consolidate them into a more cost-effective multi-stop full truckload.

scenarios.title: Select Best Parcel Carrier
scenarios.description: When packing a small e-commerce order, the system must automatically select the cheapest parcel carrier for the required service level.

scenarios.title: Update Carrier Insurance Certificate
scenarios.description: A carrier's insurance certificate is about to expire. A compliance specialist must obtain and upload the new certificate.

scenarios.title: Handle Carrier Late Arrival for Pickup
scenarios.description: A carrier scheduled for a pickup is running late. The shipping manager must assess the impact.

scenarios.title: Handle Proof of Delivery Discrepancy
scenarios.description: A customer claims they only received 9 out of 10 pallets, but the carrier's POD is signed clean.

scenarios.title: Select Cheapest LTL Carrier
scenarios.description: A planner needs to ship three pallets and must select the most cost-effective Less-Than-Truckload (LTL) carrier.

scenarios.title: Track Shipment by PRO Number
scenarios.description: A clerk needs to find the status of an LTL shipment using the carrier's progressive (PRO) number.

scenarios.title: Onboard a New Carrier
scenarios.description: A logistics company is adding a new trucking company to its network. A compliance specialist must set them up in the system.

scenarios.title: Manage Demurrage Charges
scenarios.description: A container has been left at a port terminal beyond its allotted free time, incurring demurrage fees. An agent must resolve this.

scenarios.title: Divert a Shipment In-Transit
scenarios.description: A customer needs to change the final destination of a shipment that is already on its way.

scenarios.title: Approve an Accessorial Charge
scenarios.description: A carrier adds an accessorial charge (e.g., 'Liftgate Service') to a freight bill. A specialist must review and approve it.

scenarios.title: Calculate Freight Class
scenarios.description: A shipper needs to determine the correct National Motor Freight Classification (NMFC) freight class for a new item to get accurate LTL shipping rates.

scenarios.title: Book a Guaranteed Delivery Service
scenarios.description: A critical shipment must arrive by a specific time. The planner must book a guaranteed service with an LTL carrier.

scenarios.title: Manage Driver Detention
scenarios.description: A truck driver has been waiting at a facility for over the 2-hour free time to be loaded. They report that they are now on detention.

scenarios.title: Check Customs Clearance Status
scenarios.description: An import specialist needs to check if an international shipment has been cleared by customs.

scenarios.title: Combine Multiple Parcel Shipments
scenarios.description: A customer has placed three small orders going to the same address on the same day. The system should combine them into a single, larger shipment to save on costs.

scenarios.title: File for Guaranteed Service Refund
scenarios.description: A shipment booked with a 'Guaranteed by 10 AM' service was delivered late. The company is entitled to a full refund.

scenarios.title: Receive Purchase Order Shipment
scenarios.description: A warehouse operator needs to receive an incoming shipment against a purchase order (PO), verifying item quantities and noting any discrepancies.

scenarios.title: Pick a Sales Order
scenarios.description: A warehouse operator is assigned a sales order and must pick the correct items and quantities from their storage locations.

scenarios.title: Pack and Manifest an Order
scenarios.description: A packer at a packing station needs to pack a picked order, select the correct box size, and generate a shipping label.

scenarios.title: Perform a Kitting Task
scenarios.description: An operator is assigned a work order to assemble a 'gift basket' kit from several individual items.

scenarios.title: Optimize Storage Bins via Consolidation
scenarios.description: To create space, a warehouse manager wants to consolidate partially filled storage bins of the same item into fewer bins.

scenarios.title: Receive a Cross-Dock Shipment
scenarios.description: A shipment arrives that is designated for cross-docking. It must be received and immediately moved to an outbound staging lane without being put away.

scenarios.title: Load an Outbound Truck
scenarios.description: A warehouse loader must load all staged pallets for a specific shipment onto the correct truck, in the correct order.

scenarios.title: Re-slot a High-Velocity Item
scenarios.description: An item's sales have increased, and a warehouse manager wants to move it to a forward picking location closer to the packing stations to reduce travel time.

scenarios.title: Apply Special Labeling (VAS)
scenarios.description: A specific customer requires all their outbound cartons to have a special 'Fragile' label and a customer-specific barcode label applied before shipping.

scenarios.title: Receive a Shipment Without a PO
scenarios.description: A truck arrives with a shipment that is not linked to a purchase order in the system. The operator must handle this exception.

scenarios.title: Manage a Dock Appointment
scenarios.description: A carrier calls to reschedule their delivery appointment for a later time.

scenarios.title: Conduct a Warehouse Safety Inspection
scenarios.description: A safety manager uses a checklist on a mobile device to conduct a weekly safety inspection of the warehouse.

scenarios.title: Replenish a Pick Face
scenarios.description: A forward picking location for a popular item is running low. The system must generate a task to replenish it from bulk storage.

scenarios.title: Print SSCC Pallet Labels
scenarios.description: After building a pallet for a large retail customer, the operator must generate and apply a Serial Shipping Container Code (SSCC) label for compliance.

scenarios.title: Manage Hazardous Materials Storage
scenarios.description: A shipment of hazardous materials (e.g., flammable liquids) arrives and must be stored in a designated, compliant area.

scenarios.title: Perform Cycle Count with Mobile Scanner
scenarios.description: A warehouse operator uses a handheld scanner to perform a cycle count task, guided by the system.

scenarios.title: Wave Planning for Picking
scenarios.description: A shift manager needs to create a picking wave to release a batch of orders to the floor for fulfillment.

scenarios.title: Move Inventory with a Forklift
scenarios.description: A forklift operator is assigned a task to move a full pallet of goods from a receiving lane to a bulk storage location.

scenarios.title: Assign Tasks Based on Operator Skill
scenarios.description: A task to operate a specialized piece of equipment (e.g., a turret truck) is created. The WMS must assign it only to operators certified to use that equipment.

scenarios.title: Pick to Cart for Multiple Orders
scenarios.description: An operator uses a smart cart to pick multiple small orders simultaneously in a single pass through the warehouse.

scenarios.title: Cross-Check Pallet at Loading Dock
scenarios.description: To prevent loading errors, a final check is performed by scanning a pallet's license plate at the dock door to ensure it belongs on the truck being loaded.

scenarios.title: Manage Battery Charging for MHE
scenarios.description: A forklift's battery is low. The operator must swap it for a charged one at a battery station.

scenarios.title: Perform an Outbound Quality Audit
scenarios.description: Before an order is sealed, a quality auditor randomly selects it for an audit to verify picking and packing accuracy.

scenarios.title: Direct Put-Away to Optimal Bin
scenarios.description: After receiving an item, the WMS must direct the operator to the best possible storage bin, considering space, item type, and existing stock.

scenarios.title: Handle a Pick Face Stockout
scenarios.description: A picker is directed to a location, but finds it empty. They must report the stockout so it can be resolved.

scenarios.title: Audit Picker Accuracy
scenarios.description: A manager wants to review the accuracy rates for individual pickers over the last week.

scenarios.title: Schedule Preventive Maintenance
scenarios.description: A maintenance planner needs to schedule the quarterly preventive maintenance (PM) for a critical CNC machine.

scenarios.title: Respond to an Emergency Breakdown
scenarios.description: A conveyor belt has suddenly stopped. A maintenance technician needs to respond to the corrective maintenance call.

scenarios.title: Check Spare Part Inventory
scenarios.description: A technician needs a specific hydraulic pump to repair a machine and must check if it's in the maintenance storeroom.

scenarios.title: Consult a Technical Manual for a Repair
scenarios.description: A technician is working on an unfamiliar piece of equipment and needs to access its technical service manual.

scenarios.title: Complete a PM Checklist
scenarios.description: A technician is performing a scheduled preventive maintenance task and needs to complete the associated checklist.

scenarios.title: Log a Fault Code from a Machine
scenarios.description: A machine has thrown a fault code. A technician needs to log this code in the maintenance ticket to aid in diagnosis.

scenarios.title: Order a Replacement Spare Part
scenarios.description: After using the last spare motor in stock, a technician needs to order a replacement to replenish the inventory.

scenarios.title: View Asset Maintenance History
scenarios.description: A technician is investigating a recurring problem with a machine and wants to review its entire maintenance history.

scenarios.title: Adjust a PM Schedule
scenarios.description: Based on usage data, a reliability engineer has determined that a PM task can be done less frequently. They need to adjust the schedule.

scenarios.title: Perform a Root Cause Analysis (RCA) for a Breakdown
scenarios.description: Following a major equipment failure, a reliability engineer needs to lead a formal Root Cause Analysis (RCA).

scenarios.title: Decommission an Old Asset
scenarios.description: A piece of equipment is being replaced and needs to be formally decommissioned in the asset management system.

scenarios.title: Access an Electrical Schematic
scenarios.description: A technician is troubleshooting a control panel and needs the electrical schematic for the machine.

scenarios.title: Generate a Condition-Based Maintenance Alert
scenarios.description: A sensor on a machine has detected a vibration level that exceeds the predefined threshold, indicating a potential bearing failure.

scenarios.title: Request an External Contractor for a Repair
scenarios.description: A specialized piece of equipment has broken down and requires a certified technician from the OEM to repair it.

scenarios.title: Perform a Spare Part Cycle Count
scenarios.description: A maintenance manager wants to verify the on-hand quantity of a critical, high-value spare part.

scenarios.title: Use AR for Remote Assistance
scenarios.description: A junior technician is struggling with a complex repair and uses an Augmented Reality (AR) headset to get help from a senior expert.

scenarios.title: Create a New PM Plan
scenarios.description: A new piece of equipment has been installed and a reliability engineer needs to create its preventive maintenance plan from scratch.

scenarios.title: Re-open a Maintenance Ticket
scenarios.description: A repair that was marked as 'complete' yesterday has failed again. The operator needs to re-open the original ticket.

scenarios.title: Add a New Spare Part to the System
scenarios.description: A new type of sensor will be used on several machines. It needs to be added to the spare parts inventory system.

scenarios.title: Find a Lockout-Tagout (LOTO) Procedure
scenarios.description: Before starting a repair, a technician must find the specific Lockout-Tagout (LOTO) procedure for the machine to ensure it is properly de-energized.

scenarios.title: Adjust Daily Production Schedule
scenarios.description: A shift supervisor needs to adjust the daily production schedule due to an unexpected machine breakdown.

scenarios.title: Create a New Work Order
scenarios.description: A production planner needs to create a new work order for a custom product.

scenarios.title: Respond to Real-time Production Alert
scenarios.description: An operator receives a real-time alert from a CNC machine indicating a tool is nearing its wear limit.

scenarios.title: Report Material Scrap
scenarios.description: A machine operator needs to report a quantity of raw material that was scrapped during a production run due to a defect.

scenarios.title: Expedite a Production Order
scenarios.description: A production manager needs to expedite a specific work order to meet a new customer deadline.

scenarios.title: Track Work Order Progress
scenarios.description: A production supervisor wants to check the progress of a specific high-priority work order.

scenarios.title: Adjust Machine Operating Parameters
scenarios.description: A process engineer needs to adjust the operating speed of a packaging machine to improve efficiency.

scenarios.title: Request Materials from Warehouse
scenarios.description: A line operator is running low on a specific component and needs to request more from the central warehouse.

scenarios.title: Split a Work Order
scenarios.description: A planner needs to split a large work order into two smaller batches to run on different machines simultaneously.

scenarios.title: Log Production Output
scenarios.description: An operator at the end of a shift needs to log the quantity of good parts produced for a specific work order.

scenarios.title: Handle a Machine Downtime Event
scenarios.description: A production line has stopped due to a machine fault. The supervisor needs to log the downtime event.

scenarios.title: Perform a Cycle Count
scenarios.description: A warehouse operator is tasked with performing a cycle count for a specific inventory item at a production line.

scenarios.title: Reschedule Production due to Material Shortage
scenarios.description: A planner discovers a critical raw material will be delayed and needs to reschedule all affected work orders.

scenarios.title: Close a Completed Work Order
scenarios.description: A work order has been fully completed and all units have passed final inspection. It needs to be closed.

scenarios.title: Monitor Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE)
scenarios.description: A plant manager wants to review the OEE score for the main assembly line for the previous day.

scenarios.title: Return Unused Material to Warehouse
scenarios.description: A production run is complete, and there is unused raw material at the workstation that needs to be returned to inventory.

scenarios.title: Sequence Jobs at a Workstation
scenarios.description: A supervisor needs to manually sequence the jobs queued at a specific workstation for the day to minimize setup time.

scenarios.title: Record Operator Labor
scenarios.description: An operator needs to record the time they spent working on a specific operation of a work order.

scenarios.title: Acknowledge Safety Alert
scenarios.description: A light curtain on a robotic arm has been tripped, pausing the machine. The area supervisor needs to acknowledge and resolve the alert.

scenarios.title: Move Finished Goods to Inventory
scenarios.description: A pallet of finished goods has been produced and needs to be moved from the production line to the finished goods warehouse.

scenarios.title: Record In-Process Quality Inspection
scenarios.description: A quality inspector needs to record the results of an in-process inspection for a work order.

scenarios.title: Create a Non-Conformance Report (NCR)
scenarios.description: A part has failed a quality inspection. A quality engineer needs to create a Non-Conformance Report (NCR).

scenarios.title: Prepare for an ISO 9001 Audit
scenarios.description: A quality manager needs to pull training records for all operators on a specific production line to prepare for an external audit.

scenarios.title: Trace a Component Lot Number
scenarios.description: A customer has reported an issue with a finished product. A quality engineer needs to trace the lot number of a specific component used in that product.

scenarios.title: Calibrate a Measuring Instrument
scenarios.description: A quality technician needs to perform and log the calibration of a digital caliper.

scenarios.title: Initiate a Corrective Action (CAPA)
scenarios.description: An internal audit has found a recurring process failure. A quality manager needs to initiate a formal Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) request.

scenarios.title: Manage a Controlled Document
scenarios.description: A process engineer has updated a Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) and needs to submit it for approval and release.

scenarios.title: Perform a First Article Inspection (FAI)
scenarios.description: A new product is being run for the first time. A quality inspector must perform a First Article Inspection (FAI) to validate the production process.

scenarios.title: Determine Disposition for Non-Conforming Material
scenarios.description: The Material Review Board (MRB) is meeting to decide the disposition for a batch of non-conforming parts.

scenarios.title: Perform Product Genealogy Search
scenarios.description: To investigate a quality trend, an engineer needs to find all finished products that were made using a specific lot of raw material.

scenarios.title: Conduct a Supplier Audit
scenarios.description: A supplier quality engineer is conducting an on-site audit at a supplier's facility and needs to log their findings.

scenarios.title: Release Product from Quality Hold
scenarios.description: A batch of products was held for inspection and has now been cleared. A quality technician needs to release it for shipment.

scenarios.title: Track CAPA Effectiveness
scenarios.description: A CAPA was implemented 3 months ago. A quality manager needs to check its effectiveness by reviewing recent data.

scenarios.title: Acknowledge new version of a compliance document
scenarios.description: A new version of a safety procedure has been released. An operator needs to acknowledge that they have read and understood it.

scenarios.title: Generate a Certificate of Analysis (CoA)
scenarios.description: A batch of product has passed final testing and needs a Certificate of Analysis (CoA) to be sent with the shipment.

scenarios.title: Perform Statistical Process Control (SPC) Analysis
scenarios.description: A quality engineer is monitoring a critical process parameter and wants to analyze its SPC chart.

scenarios.title: Extend a CAPA Due Date
scenarios.description: The team investigating a CAPA needs more time for their root cause analysis. A manager needs to approve an extension.

scenarios.title: Link a Customer Complaint to an NCR
scenarios.description: A customer complaint has been received that matches an existing internal Non-Conformance Report. The two records need to be linked.

scenarios.title: Verify Supplier Certificate of Conformance (CoC)
scenarios.description: A shipment has been received with a supplier's Certificate of Conformance (CoC). An inspector needs to verify it.

scenarios.title: Track Serialized Components
scenarios.description: An assembly requires several critical, serialized components. An operator needs to scan and associate each component's serial number with the main assembly's serial number.

scenarios.title: Create a New Customer Quote
scenarios.description: A sales representative needs to create a quote for a potential new customer for a standard product.

scenarios.title: Check Order Status
scenarios.description: A customer calls to ask for the latest status of their open order.

scenarios.title: Handle a Product Quality Complaint
scenarios.description: A customer has received a product that is defective and wants a replacement.

scenarios.title: Update a Customer Account
scenarios.description: A customer has a new shipping address and it needs to be updated in their account.

scenarios.title: Convert a Quote to a Sales Order
scenarios.description: A customer has accepted a quote and wants to proceed with the order.

scenarios.title: Provide a Shipment Tracking Number
scenarios.description: A customer is asking for the tracking number for their recent shipment.

scenarios.title: Resolve a Shipping Error
scenarios.description: A customer received the wrong product. A customer service rep needs to resolve the issue.

scenarios.title: Renew a Customer's Annual Contract
scenarios.description: A major customer's annual supply contract is due for renewal next month.

scenarios.title: Create a Quote for a Custom Product
scenarios.description: A sales rep is working with engineering to create a quote for a new, custom-configured product.

scenarios.title: Check Production Capacity for a Large Order
scenarios.description: A customer wants to place a very large order, and the sales rep needs to check if there is enough production capacity to meet the requested delivery date.

scenarios.title: Handle a Request for a Certificate of Conformance
scenarios.description: A customer requires a Certificate of Conformance (CoC) for a product they have already received.

scenarios.title: Onboard a New Customer Account
scenarios.description: A new customer has placed their first order. An account manager needs to set up their full account profile.

scenarios.title: Cancel a Sales Order
scenarios.description: A customer needs to cancel an order that has been placed but has not yet entered production.

scenarios.title: Inform Customer of a Production Delay
scenarios.description: A production delay means a customer's order will be late. A sales rep needs to proactively inform the customer.

scenarios.title: Resolve an Invoice Dispute
scenarios.description: A customer is disputing an item on their invoice, claiming they were overcharged.

scenarios.title: Update a Customer's Pricing Agreement
scenarios.description: A customer has negotiated a new, lower price for a high-volume product. This needs to be updated in their contract.

scenarios.title: Amend a Sales Order
scenarios.description: A customer wants to increase the quantity of an item on an order that has not yet entered production.

scenarios.title: Provide Proof of Delivery (POD)
scenarios.description: A customer's finance department requires a signed Proof of Delivery (POD) for a shipment before they can pay the invoice.

scenarios.title: Process a Customer Return (RMA)
scenarios.description: A customer is returning a product they ordered by mistake. A customer service rep needs to process the return.

scenarios.title: Add a New Contact to a Customer Account
scenarios.description: A new engineer at a customer company needs to be added as a technical contact on their account.

scenarios.title: Create a Purchase Order for Raw Materials
scenarios.description: A buyer needs to create a purchase order (PO) for a critical raw material that is below its reorder point.

scenarios.title: Log a Received Shipment
scenarios.description: A receiving clerk needs to log an incoming shipment of components from a supplier.

scenarios.title: Arrange Shipping for a Customer Order
scenarios.description: A logistics coordinator needs to arrange transportation for a completed customer order.

scenarios.title: Resolve a Supplier Delivery Delay
scenarios.description: A buyer is notified by a supplier that a critical shipment will be delayed. They need to assess the impact.

scenarios.title: Qualify a New Supplier
scenarios.description: A procurement manager needs to add a new potential supplier to the system for a specific commodity.

scenarios.title: Handle a Shipment Discrepancy
scenarios.description: A receiving clerk received a shipment where the quantity is less than what was on the packing slip.

scenarios.title: Track an Inbound Shipment
scenarios.description: A planner wants to know the current location and ETA of a critical inbound shipment.

scenarios.title: Generate a Packing Slip
scenarios.description: A shipping clerk has picked all items for a customer order and needs to generate the packing slip.

scenarios.title: Conduct a Supplier Performance Review
scenarios.description: A category manager needs to pull performance data for a key supplier for a quarterly business review.

scenarios.title: Request a Quote from Multiple Suppliers
scenarios.description: A buyer is sourcing a new component and needs to send a Request for Quote (RFQ) to several potential suppliers.

scenarios.title: Schedule a Receiving Appointment
scenarios.description: A supplier needs to schedule a delivery appointment for a large shipment arriving next week.

scenarios.title: Consolidate Multiple Orders into One Shipment
scenarios.description: A logistics planner has multiple orders going to the same customer and wants to consolidate them into a single, larger shipment to save on freight costs.
